### 🔵 Volume {.unnumbered}

::: lt-grid

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c1}
LT1
:::
::: lt-title
Volume — Prisms
:::
::: lt-desc
Calculate the volume of right prisms using $V = A_{\text{base}} \times l$, including rectangular, triangular, trapezoidal, and parallelogram cross-sections.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Cross-section area]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Prism formula]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Mixed cross-sections]{.lt-skill .c1}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c2}
LT2
:::
::: lt-title
Volume — Cylinders
:::
::: lt-desc
Calculate the volume of a full cylinder using $V = \pi r^2 h$, given either radius or diameter.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Circle area]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Radius vs diameter]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Cylinder formula]{.lt-skill .c2}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c3}
LT3
:::
::: lt-title
Volume — Portions
:::
::: lt-desc
Find volumes of partial cylinders: half $\tfrac{1}{2}\pi r^2 h$, quarter, and sector $\tfrac{\theta}{360}\pi r^2 h$.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Half cylinder]{.lt-skill .c3}
[Sector fraction]{.lt-skill .c3}
[Angle proportion]{.lt-skill .c3}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c4}
LT4
:::
::: lt-title
Volume — Reverse
:::
::: lt-desc
Rearrange volume formulas to find unknown dimensions such as length, height, or radius.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Rearrange formula]{.lt-skill .c4}
[Unknown dimension]{.lt-skill .c4}
[Algebraic substitution]{.lt-skill .c4}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c5}
LT5
:::
::: lt-title
Volume — Context
:::
::: lt-desc
Solve real-world problems involving volume, including converting m³ to litres.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Word problems]{.lt-skill .c5}
[Unit conversion]{.lt-skill .c5}
[m³ to litres]{.lt-skill .c5}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c1}
LT6
:::
::: lt-title
Composite Volume
:::
::: lt-desc
Break complex solids into simpler shapes and add or subtract their volumes.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Decompose solids]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Add & subtract]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Multi-step]{.lt-skill .c1}
:::
:::

:::

---

### 🟢 Surface Area {.unnumbered}

::: lt-grid

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c2}
LT7
:::
::: lt-title
SA — Prisms
:::
::: lt-desc
Calculate surface area of cuboids, triangular and trapezoidal prisms, and open boxes.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Net of prism]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Face areas]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Open box]{.lt-skill .c2}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c3}
LT8
:::
::: lt-title
SA — Cylinders
:::
::: lt-desc
Calculate surface area of closed cylinders, open-top cylinders, tubes, and half-cylinders.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Curved surface]{.lt-skill .c3}
[Open vs closed]{.lt-skill .c3}
[Half cylinder]{.lt-skill .c3}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c4}
LT9
:::
::: lt-title
SA — Composite
:::
::: lt-desc
Find surface area of composite solids, including faces that are added or removed at joins.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Composite solids]{.lt-skill .c4}
[Remove shared faces]{.lt-skill .c4}
[Multi-step]{.lt-skill .c4}
:::
:::

:::

---

### 🟣 Advanced & Algebra {.unnumbered}

::: lt-grid

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c5}
LT10
:::
::: lt-title
Unit Conversions
:::
::: lt-desc
Convert between length, area, and volume units using the correct powers of conversion factors.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Length units]{.lt-skill .c5}
[Area units ×²]{.lt-skill .c5}
[Volume units ×³]{.lt-skill .c5}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c1}
LT11
:::
::: lt-title
Pyramids, Cones & Spheres
:::
::: lt-desc
Apply $V=\tfrac{1}{3}Ah$, $V=\tfrac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$, $SA=\pi r^2+\pi rl$, $V=\tfrac{4}{3}\pi r^3$, and $SA=4\pi r^2$, including composite solids.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Pyramid]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Cone]{.lt-skill .c1}
[Sphere]{.lt-skill .c1}
:::
:::

::: lt-card
::: {.lt-badge .c2}
LT12
:::
::: lt-title
Algebraic Volume
:::
::: lt-desc
Work with algebraic dimensions — expand and simplify expressions, or solve for $x$ given a volume.
:::
::: lt-skills
[Expand & simplify]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Solve for x]{.lt-skill .c2}
[Algebraic dimensions]{.lt-skill .c2}
:::
:::

:::
